stories.headline: Drones, Iran war escalating horror as Sudan war enters fourth year
stories.summary: UN says hundreds killed by drone strikes over past three months, as medical charity reports two more deaths in Darfur.

stories.headline: It is time for a ceasefire in Sudan and a new way forward
stories.summary: The SAF and the RSF cannot shape the future of Sudan alone. All political actors and the Sudanese people must join.

stories.headline: US to Let Iran Oil Sanctions Waiver Expire Amid Blockade
stories.summary: The Trump administration will allow a waiver temporarily authorizing the purchase of certain Iranian crude oil to expire this weekend.

stories.headline: U.S. blockade has turned back six merchant ships leaving Strait of Hormuz
stories.summary: More than a dozen American warships positioned in the Gulf of Oman and Arabian Sea are acting as a “net,” officials said. None of the encounters has required escalation.

stories.headline: Hamas rejects Gaza disarmament plan, Palestinian official says
stories.summary: The official tells the BBC that the armed group will not move forward with talks until Israel fully complies with its commitments.

stories.summary: Ukrainian leader hopes for ‘pragmatic’ and ‘friendly’ relations with new government in contrast with hostility of previous pro-Russian regimein BerlinAt his press conference with Zelenskyy, Germany’s chancellor Friedrich Merz also welcomed Péter Magyar’s decisive victory, saying it would have “implications for our support for Ukraine”.“More Hungarians than ever before cast their votes. By an overwhelming majority, they voted not only to oust a go

stories.headline: How many ships are crossing the Strait of Hormuz?
stories.summary: Four vessels with links to Iran have crossed the Strait of Hormuz despite the start of a US naval blockade, but two of those vessels appear to have reversed course.

stories.headline: Brazil's former spy chief detained by ICE agents in US
stories.summary: Alexandre Ramagem was sentenced to 16 years in jail for plotting a military coup in Brazil but had already fled the country.

stories.headline: Tankers transiting Strait of Hormuz stop or turn around amid US blockade, data shows

stories.headline: Fed chair nominee Kevin Warsh reveals assets worth more than $130mn
stories.summary: Trump’s pick to succeed Jay Powell reports $10mn in consulting fees from Stanley Druckenmiller’s family office

stories.headline: No ships ‘make it past US blockade’ in Hormuz strait in first day: Pentagon
stories.summary: CENTCOM contradicts reports on ships breaking blockade as Trump says US-Iran talks could resume in the next two days.

stories.headline: Manchin Warns of The Historic Rise of US Debt As Iran War Drags On
stories.summary: Former Senator Joe Manchin said continued attempts in Congress to use party-line votes to pass spending legislation in Congress part of the reason the federal debt is ballooning. He joined Bloomberg's Balance of Power to discuss.  (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Warsh Fed Chair Hearing Set for Next Week, Tim Scott Says
stories.summary: The Senate Banking Committee has scheduled a confirmation hearing at 10 a.m. on April 21 for Kevin Warsh, President Donald Trump’s nominee for Federal Reserve chair.

stories.headline: Zelenskyy pitches new joint security system to European allies
stories.summary: Ukraine should be an integral part of Europe’s security, or some on the continent would be part of the "Russian world," Zelenskyy said as he announced discussions over a new joint defence system with EU and non-EU countries.

stories.headline: China Evergrande’s billionaire boss pleads guilty to fraud
stories.summary: Hui Ka Yan expresses remorse in trial proceedings after collapse of world’s most indebted property developerBusiness live – latest updatesA former steelworker who rose to become one of China’s richest people has pleaded guilty to charges including fundraising fraud after the collapse of Evergrande, the world’s most indebted property developer.The property group’s founder, Hui Ka Yan, “pleaded guilty and expressed remorse” in trial proceedings at

stories.headline: Watch: What are Harry and Meghan doing in Australia?
stories.summary: BBC News journalist Simon Atkinson explains how the couple's tour will be different compared to their last visit.

stories.headline: Founder of China's Evergrande pleads guilty to fraud
stories.summary: Evergrande was once China's biggest real estate firm, with a stock market valuation of more than $50bn.

stories.headline: Trump says new US-Iran talks could happen ‘over next two days’
stories.summary: The US president said additional talks could happen this week in Islamabad but that further discussions to end the war would happen in Europe

stories.headline: Fix to critical UK jobs data faces further delays
stories.summary: ONS is privately warning that it will not iron out problems in its ‘transformed’ labour force survey before 2027

stories.headline: Deutsche Bank’s Saravelos Says Sell Dollar as War Risks Peak
stories.summary: The risks linked to the war in Iran will be subsiding and it is time to bet on a weaker US dollar, according to Deutsche Bank AG.

stories.headline: A New Accuser Says Eric Swalwell Sexually Assaulted Her
stories.summary: The woman said Mr. Swalwell, who resigned from Congress on Tuesday afternoon, raped her in a West Hollywood hotel room in 2018. She said she believed she was drugged.

stories.headline: Trump Administration Accuses Biden DOJ of Unfairly Prosecuting Anti-Abortion Activists
stories.summary: The report is part of the president’s effort to claim anti-conservative and anti-Christian biases in federal law enforcement, even as he pushes to wield the legal system against his political enemies.

stories.headline: Trump and MAGA Embraced Orban. Now, They’re Worried by His Loss in Hungary’s Election.
stories.summary: Viktor Orban, the Hungarian prime minister, and his right-wing playbook were embraced by parts of the American right. Now some are worried by his defeat.

stories.headline: Tony Gonzales, a Texas Congressman, Says He Will Resign
stories.summary: The third-term Republican lawmaker is accused of coercing a staff member into a sexual relationship.

stories.headline: Mark Carney secures majority government in Canada after special election win
stories.summary: Carney’s Liberals will now be able to pass legislation without the support of opposition parties – and govern until 2029The Canadian prime minister, Mark Carney, has secured a parliamentary majority for his Liberal government, CBC News reported. The victory will help him push through a legislative agenda he says is needed for an increasingly divided geopolitical world.Three special elections were held on Monday in Ontario and Quebec, with two in

stories.headline: JD Vance defends backing 'great guy' Orbán's campaign after landslide defeat
stories.summary: Hungary's leader was a "great guy" who did a "very good job", the US vice-president said, but he was sure he could work with Péter Magyar.

stories.headline: Bongbong Marcos fights ill-health rumours with star jumps
stories.summary: The Philippine President challenged anyone questioning his fitness to join him in the gym.

stories.headline: Spain approves plan to give around 500,000 undocumented migrants legal status
stories.summary: Prime Minister Pedro Sánchez said migrants helped "build the rich, open and diverse Spain that we are and to which we aspire".

stories.headline: Harry and Meghan mix charity and business on Australia visit
stories.summary: There are questions over whether local taxpayers are picking up any of the bill for policing the visit.

stories.headline: BlackRock profits jump as it draws in $130bn
stories.summary: Earnings for first quarter underline asset manager’s push into investment products that generate higher fees

stories.summary: Data highlights concerns about lack of jobs growth among British-born population

stories.headline: Latham partners pocket record $8.7mn average pay

stories.headline: Goldman Sachs Private Credit Fund Sells $750 Million Bond
stories.summary: A Goldman Sachs Group Inc. private credit fund raised $750 million from a bond sale on Tuesday, making it the second private credit fund to tap the investment-grade debt market this week.

stories.headline: Wells Fargo CFO Sees Growth Across Business Lines
stories.summary: Wells Fargo CFO Mike Santomassimo breaks down the bank's first-quarter earnings and discusses growth prospects and the private credit market on "Bloomberg The Close." (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: A Tax Day Cash Drain Is Coming for Calm US Funding Markets
stories.summary: Wall Street strategists expect a robust increase in the Treasury’s coffers this week as Americans pay their taxes, potentially putting pressure on relatively calm US funding markets.

stories.headline: Bond Market Veteran Bassman Sues ETF Manager Over Compensation
stories.summary: Harley Bassman, who invented a widely-followed gauge of Treasury-market volatility and later developed exchange-traded funds for Simplify Asset Management, is suing the firm for allegedly stiffing him of millions of dollars in compensation.

stories.headline: US Natural Gas Extends Losses on Oil Price Dive, Mixed Forecasts
stories.summary: US natural gas futures ended lower for a fifth consecutive session, erasing earlier gains as traders weighed plunging oil prices against mixed weather outlooks. Traders may also have more readily unwound long positions as forecasts later in April show weeks of mild weather despite a slight cold shift.

stories.headline: Fintech SumUp Lines Up Banks for Possible London IPO
stories.summary: SumUp Payments Ltd. is lining up investment banks to arrange a potential initial public offering in London, according to people familiar with the matter, in what could be one of the biggest listings the UK capital has seen in recent years.

stories.headline: U.S. Military Strikes Another Boat in Pacific, Killing 2
stories.summary: The attack was the 49th since the campaign against vessels in the Caribbean and the eastern Pacific started in early September.

stories.headline: UK steel exports to EU at risk as bloc doubles tariffs and halves quotas
stories.summary: Decision to reduce duty-free quotas by 47% aimed at curbing Chinese importsThe EU is to go ahead with plans to double tariffs and halve quotas on imports of steel from July, in a move designed to curb Chinese imports but which could damage UK exports to the bloc.The decision by EU lawmakers and member states after late night talks on Monday, will reduce duty-free quotas by 47%. Exact country allocations have yet to be determined. Continue reading

stories.headline: EU auditors question new fund backed by EU carbon border tax for polluting industries
stories.summary: EU auditors argue the Temporary Decarbonisation Fund, designed to channel revenues from the EU's carbon border tax to help heavy industries cope with the energy transition, may do little to drive new green investment.

stories.headline: Oldest gorilla in captivity celebrates 69th birthday in Berlin Zoo
stories.summary: Arriving in Berlin in 1959, the western lowland gorilla has far outlived the typical 35-40 year wild lifespan through specialised care.

stories.headline: As Iran war drags on, midterm forecasts for Republicans get even worse
stories.summary: The GOP was already bracing for a tough election, but with the ceasefire in doubt, gas prices are likely to stay high, and voters’ economic outlook is dismal.

stories.headline: Israel steps up strikes on Lebanon and Hezbollah attacks continue as talks loom
stories.summary: More than 160 people have been killed since Friday in Lebanon, where Israel is pressing on with its campaign against Hezbollah despite the US-Iran ceasefire.

stories.headline: Hungary's next PM would pick up if Putin calls and tell him to stop Ukraine war
stories.summary: Russia has offered "pragmatic" relations, but Péter Magyar says he will not call Vladimir Putin himself.

stories.headline: Eric Swalwell to resign from Congress after sexual misconduct claims
stories.summary: Four women have accused the congressman and ex-candidate for California governor of misconduct ranging from sexual harassment to rape, which he has denied.

stories.headline: Orbán era swept away by Péter Magyar's Hungary election landslide
stories.summary: Viktor Orbán's 16-year rule is over, defeated by a 45-year-old ex-party insider who convinced a majority of Hungarians to oust him.

stories.headline: Ex-Nigerian oil minister denies taking bribes
stories.summary: Diezani Alison-Madueke is accused of living “a life of luxury” in the UK at properties paid for and refurbished by industry figures.

stories.headline: Philippines accuses Chinese fishermen of dumping cyanide in South China Sea
stories.summary: Beijing rejects the allegation as a "farce", while Manila claims the poison was used to kill fish and deprive troops stationed in the area of food.

stories.headline: UK could adopt EU single market rules under new legislation
stories.summary: The move has raised questions over parliamentary scrutiny of future rules to deliver planned EU deals.

stories.headline: How Trump’s Jesus-like image and feud with the Pope are sparking backlash
stories.summary: BBC's Sarah Smith looks at how the president's most ardent supporters are reacting to his latest social media posts.

stories.headline: Ukraine and Russia accuse each other of hundreds of ceasefire violations
stories.summary: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky says his nation's forces would respond "symmetrically" to Russian attacks.

stories.headline: Hungary’s Viktor Orban, ally of Trump and Putin, concedes election defeat
stories.summary: WIth record turnout, Hungarians chose to end the 16-year rule of the prime minister who was a self-proclaimed champion of illiberal Christian democracy.

stories.headline: Political turmoil in Indian border state as nine million lose voting rights
stories.summary: Nine million voters have been dropped from rolls in West Bengal, raising concerns over exclusion and fairness.

stories.headline: Lebanon thought there was a ceasefire - then Israel unleashed deadly blitz
stories.summary: Israel says Lebanon is not included in the ceasefire the US agreed to halt the war with Iran.

stories.headline: Hot in the city: Energy crisis tests Singapore's air-con addiction
stories.summary: The rise in energy prices has hit Asia particularly hard as many nations are heavily reliant on Gulf oil.

stories.headline: Argentina passes bill loosening protection of its glaciers
stories.summary: The new bill will make it easier for provincial governments to allow mining projects in glacier regions.

stories.headline: Iran ceasefire deal gives Trump a way out of war - but at a high cost
stories.summary: The path to the two-week ceasefire with Iran may have fundamentally altered the way the rest of the world views the US.

stories.headline: Why Real Madrid tie could make or break Kane's Ballon d'Or hopes
stories.summary: Harry Kane is the leading goalscorer in Europe but does he need to win a major trophy to have a chance of winning the prestigious award?

stories.headline: The US refinery now processing Venezuelan oil
stories.summary: Chevron is now importing 250,000 barrels of crude per day from Venezuela.

stories.headline: Carnival fever hits Lagos as locals celebrate Afro-Brazilian heritage
stories.summary: The centuries-old Fanti Carnival returned to Nigeria on Monday with a burst of colour and spectacle.
